title: Clerk middleware placed in wrong directory — auth silently does nothing
summary: Agent puts middleware.ts inside app/ directory instead of project root or src/. Clerk auth appears to work in dev but silently fails to protect routes.
mistake: The agent creates `app/middleware.ts` instead of `middleware.ts` (project root) or `src/middleware.ts`. Next.js only reads middleware from the root or src/ — placing it inside `app/` means it's never executed. The app loads without errors, login pages appear to work, but protected routes are not actually protected. The agent doesn't notice because there's no error message — the middleware simply never fires.
instead: ALWAYS place middleware.ts at the project root (`./middleware.ts`) or inside `src/` if using the src directory pattern (`./src/middleware.ts`). NEVER place it inside `app/`. After creating the file, verify it's active by checking that accessing a protected route without auth redirects to the sign-in page. The middleware matcher in the config must also cover the routes you want to protect.
